questions.question: A payslip shows: Gross pay £2,100 | Income tax (20%) £420 | National Insurance (12%) £252 | Net pay: £1,428. What is 'net pay'?
A bank statement shows 'DR £450'. DR means debit — money leaving your account. What has happened?
An energy bill says 'Direct Debit of £95 p.m.' What does this mean?
options: £95 is automatically taken from your bank each month
questions.answer: £95 is automatically taken from your bank each month

questions.question: Gross pay: £2,100. Tax: 20%. NI: 12%. What is net pay?
You have £3,500 savings. Interest rate is 3% per year. How much interest do you earn after 1 year?
Energy bill: £95/month. It rises by 18%. What is the new monthly cost?
